free_text: Neutron scattering from Mo-94 and Mo-96
Abstract is given.
Comments and data are given.
JAERI 5.5 MeV accelerator and
a mobley type ns ion buncher.
4 Neutron detectors were used simultaneously.
NE213 liquid scintillator (5in.diam.*5 in.high)
and an RCA C70133B phototube.
To derive cr.sect.,The following quantities were used.
Sct. cr.sect. from hydrogen,number of atoms in the
sample, number of hydrogen atoms in a polyethylene
scatterer, counts in tof spectra,and relative
efficiency of detector.
Compared with calculations using the coupled-channel
optical model theory.
Life-time correction for electronic circuits.
Multiple scattering and attenuation of neutrons in
the sample and source-to-sample finite size effect.
Statistical errors, the errors in the
relative efficiency of the detectors and the errors
due to the multiple scattering correction.
Data taken from Tanaka 1978/03/09.
Subent 004 corrected.
SD:Updated to new date formats,lower case.
Corrections in all Subents. SAMPLE moved to Sub.002-005
Mo-94 metal right cylinder,2cm diam.*2.2 cm high.
SD: SAMPLE added.
Residual nucleus was left on the .871 MeV level (2+).
Mo-94 metal right cylinder,2cm diam.*2.2 cm high.
SD: E-EXC -> E-LVL. SPIN J, PARITY
deleted. SAMPLE added.

SD: SAMPLE added.

diam,6.5 cm high),with 16 impurity of mass.
